  • Open

    Wingstop Welcomes New Hot Honey Trio
    Wingstop expands its hot honey offerings with the launch of its new Hot Honey Trio, which includes the chain's regular Hot Honey Rub joined by two new, limited-time flavors. Available nationwide now, Wingstop's Hot Honey Trio includes: - Hot Honey Rub - "Sweet honey in a fiery dry rub." - Saucy Sriracha Hot Honey - "Hot honey gets saucy - Sriracha style." - Sweet Garlic Hot Honey - "Sticky-sweet garlic with a spicy kiss." According to Wingstop's heat scale, both Hot Honey Rub and Sweet Garlic Hot Honey measure in at three flames (out of five), while Saucy Sriracha Hot Honey comes in at four flames. As with the rest of the chain's flavors, the Hot Honey Trio can be selected for any of Wingstop's chicken offerings. Photo via Wingstop. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    The Chicken Supreme is Back at Jack in the Box
    The Chicken Supreme is back for a limited time at Jack in the Box. It's joined by new Jibbis collectible bag charms to celebrate the fast-food chain's 75th anniversary. The Chicken Supreme was first introduced in 1980 and discontinued in 2004. It consists of a crispy-fried chicken patty, Swiss-style cheese, lettuce, tomato, and mayo-onion sauce, served on a toasted wheat bun. A Double version is also available with a second chicken patty and an additional slice of cheese. Pricing for the Chicken Supreme starts at $3.49. A Chicken Supreme combo meal with small fries and a drink starts at $5, while the Double Chicken Supreme combo meal starts at $7. Finally, there's the Double Chicken Supreme Munchie Meal (pictured), which starts at $14. It includes a Double Chicken Supreme, two tacos, seasoned curly fries, and a regular drink. The meal also includes a limited-edition Jibbi bag charm. There are four different Jibbis in all. Here's what they look like: Photos via Jack in the Box. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Starbucks 2025-2026 Winter Menu Includes New Caramel Protein, Dubai Chocolate, and More
    Starbucks' 2025-2026 winter menu has arrived, featuring two new Caramel Protein beverages and two new Dubai Chocolate drinks, among other food and drinks. Here's what you'll find featured on the menu at Starbucks this winter: - New Caramel Protein Matcha - A permanent menu addition featuring matcha with protein-boosted milk and classic or sugar-free caramel syrup. It's available hot or iced and contains up to 28 to 31 grams of protein per Grande. - New Caramel Protein Latte - A permanent menu addition featuring Starbucks Espresso Roast, protein-boosted milk, and caramel syrup. It's available hot or iced and contains up to 27-29 grams of protein per Grande. - New Iced Dubai Chocolate Matcha (available for a limited time only) - Matcha, pistachio sauce, milk, and ice, topped with chocolate …  ( 16 min )
    Southern Fried Shrimp and Giant Quesadillas Return to Zaxbys
    Citing popular demand, Zaxbys brings back Southern Fried Shrimp and Giant Quesadillas for a limited time. Zaxbys' Southern Fried Shrimp is available in three options: - ZaxSeas Surf & Turf Meal - Eight Southern Fried Shrimp, Zax Sauce, three Chicken Fingerz, Texas Toast, Crinkle Fries, and a small drink. - Southern Fried Shrimp Meal - Eight Southern Fried Shrimp, Zax Sauce, Texas Toast, Crinkle Fries, and a small drink. - Southern Fried Shrimp Side - Eight shrimp with Zax Sauce. Prices vary, but a location in Atlanta charges $14.99 for the Surf & Turf Meal, $11.49 for the Southern Fried Shrimp Meal, and $6.99 for eight shrimp. There are two Giant Quesadillas to choose from: - Chicken Bacon Ranch Quesadilla - Three hand-breaded Chicken Fingerz, Cheddar Jack cheese, hardwood-smoked bacon, and ranch dressing, folded into a warm flour tortilla. It's served with a side of ranch and a small drink. - Chicken Fajita Quesadilla - Three hand-breaded Chicken Fingerz, Cheddar Jack cheese, grilled onions and peppers, and Southwest Ranch, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. It comes with a side of ranch and a small drink. The same location sells both quesadillas for $11.49 each. Photo via Zaxbys. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Jersey Mike's Launches New Mike's Hot Italian Sub
    Jersey Mike's introduces the new Mike's Hot Italian sub as a limited-time special. Selling for a discounted price of $8.95 for a regular size ($16.95 for a Giant), Mike's Hot Italian features ham, salami, and pepperoni grilled and chopped with melted provolone cheese, and then topped with grilled onions, lettuce, tomato, and chopped pepper relish on your choice of bread. It was tested in four markets late last year. Photo via Jersey Mike's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Chick-fil-A Tests New Cream Cold Brew
    Chick-fil-A is testing new Iced Coffee flavors and new Cream Cold Brew at participating restaurants in the cities of Anniston, Birmingham, and Tuscaloosa in Alabama. The Iced Coffee is being tested with new Caramel and Mocha flavors in addition to the current Vanilla and Plain options. The new Cream Cold Brew features the chain's cold-brewed coffee (same as its iced coffee) mixed with a sweet cream blend (the chain's milkshake base, according to the ingredients list) and served over ice. Like the Iced Coffee, it is available in Caramel, Mocha, Vanilla, and Plain varieties. The coffee beverage test runs through January 31, 2026, while supplies last. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    McDonald's Updates Extra Value Meal Deals
    McDonald's updates its Extra Value Meal deals by offering the $5 Sausage McMuffin with Egg Meal and the $8 Two Snack Wrap Meal for a limited time at participating locations. The $5 Sausage McMuffin with Egg Meal includes a Sausage McMuffin with Egg, Hash Browns, and a small coffee. The $8 Two Snack Wrap Meal includes two Snack Wraps (two Ranch, Two Spicy, or one of each), medium fries, and a medium fountain drink. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Our Honest Review of GHOST Welch’s Cranberry Grape Energy
    As Sporked‘s most prolific energy drink reviewer, I try to use the official nomenclature for drink brands whenever I can. It’s not “Alani”, it’s Alani Nu. It’s not Arizona, it’s AriZona.     Crumbl Bakes New Dubai Chocolate Cookies and More
    Crumbl bakes new Dubai Chocolate Cookies as part of this week's rotating dessert menu available from January 19 through January 24, 2026. The Crumbl offers a rotating menu of four desserts each week, along with a permanent Classic menu. Here's what this week's limited-time options include: - New Dubai Chocolate Cookie (pictured) - A brownie cookie layered with a crunchy kataifi and pistachio filling, finished with milk chocolate and a drizzle of pistachio cream. - Brown Sugar Cinnamon Cookie featuring Pop-Tarts - A warm brown sugar cinnamon-stuffed cookie topped with a brown sugar cream cheese glaze and crumbled Pop-Tarts. - Strawberry Crumb Cake Cookie - A buttery strawberry cookie with white drops, topped with a milky glaze and strawberry vanilla streusel. - Peanut Butter & Jelly Cookie - A warm peanut butter cookie topped with swirls of peanut butter mousse and raspberry topping. The cookie bakery chain is also offering Chocolate Chip Cookie Thins on Thursdays only. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Applebee's Serves Up New O-M-Cheese Burger
    Applebee's welcomes the new O-M-Cheese Burger to kick off 2026. Priced at a suggested $11.99, the new burger is served in a sizzling skillet with a pool of melted queso and Cheddar cheeses. The burger consists of a beef patty, American cheese, applewood-smoked bacon, and spicy honey mustard on a toasted bun. Classic fries are included on the side. The O-M-Cheese Burger is also featured as an entree option of Applebee's 2 for $25 Menu, alongside Fiesta Lime Chicken and the Oriental Chicken Salad. In addition to the new burger, $6 Mucho Smoocho Sips are back for a limited time. The discounted Valentine's Day-themed cocktails include the Tipsy Cupid and the new Prickly Ever After Rita. They're joined by the new Prickly Melon Rush Mocktail. The Tipsy Cupid features Smirnoff Vodka, orange liqueur, strawberry, and premium lemon sour, garnished with gummy lips. The New Prickly Ever After Rita features Cuervo Tradicional Blanco Tequila, orange liqueur, lime, and prickly pear, garnished with gummy lips. The Prickly Melon Rush Mocktail is a mix of Red Bull Watermelon, prickly pear, lime juice, and lemon lime soda. Photo via Applebee's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    New Ham & Three Cheese Melt and More Arrive at Jimmy John's
    Jimmy John's adds the new Ham & Three Cheese Melt as a permanent menu item. The new sandwich is joined by the limited-time return of Kickin' Ranch Chips and Red Velvet Cookies.  Also, the chain is running a limited-time promotion where you can add chips and a drink to any sandwich or wrap purchase for $1. Jimmy John's new Ham & Three Cheese Melt is a toasted sub that features a double portion of ham served with cheddar, provolone, parmesan, and mayo on French bread. Kickin' Ranch Chips are potato chips with a "zesty ranch flavor and bold pepper kick," while the Red Velvet Cookie is made with dark brown sugar, cocoa powder, a hint of vanilla, and sweet white chips. Prices vary, but a regular Ham & Three Cheese Melt costs $11.69 at my local Jimmy John's here in Los Angeles. At the same location, a bag of Kickin' Ranch Chips is $3.29 and a Red Velvet Cookie costs $3.49. Photo via Jimmy John's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    12 Arizona Pizza Spots Baking Pies So Crisp The First Bite Snaps
    You can hear it before you taste it, that clean crack when a perfectly blistered crust gives way. Arizona is secretly a paradise for pie lovers who crave leopard spotting, airy rims, and bottoms baked to a golden snap.   • Open

    Sonic Introduces New $6 All-American Smasher Meal
    Sonic starts off the new year on the value trail with the introduction of the new $6 All-American Smasher Meal for a limited time at participating locations nationwide. The value-priced combo meal includes: An All-American Sonic Smasher Medium Tots or Groovy Fries. Medium Soft Drink or Iced Tea. The All-American Sonic Smasher is a new, limited-time variant of the Sonic Smasher, featuring two made-to-order, smashed Angus beef patties served with American cheese, shredded lettuce, tomato, crinkle-cut pickles, diced onions, ketchup, and mayo on a soft potato bun. It is also available as the All-American Triple Sonic Smasher (with three beef patties), All-American Bacon Sonic Smasher (with bacon), and the All-American Triple Bacon Sonic Smasher (with three beef patties and bacon). Other limited-time menu items at Sonic right now include: Cherry Flavor Bubble Slush - A cherry slush with blue raspberry flavor bubbles. Green Apple Flavor Bubble Slush - A green apple slush with blue raspberry flavor bubbles. Salted Caramel Toffee Croissant Bites - Flaky Croissant Bites tossed in salted caramel and toffee sugar. Photo via Sonic. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Taco Bell Reveals New Luxe Value Menu
    Taco Bell is updating its Cravings Value Menu and renaming it the new Luxe Value Menu starting January 22, 2026. Taco Bell Rewards Members get early access to the new menu now when ordering through the Taco Bell mobile app or website. The Luxe Value Menu features 10 items priced at $3 or less, including five new dishes. The new items include: - New Mini Taco Salad (suggested price of $2.49) - A crispy tortilla bowl filled with seasoned beef, creamy Chipotle Sauce, cheddar cheese, lettuce, tomatoes, and refried beans. - New Beefy Potato Loaded Griller (suggested price of $2.49) - Seasoned ground beef, Creamy Chipotle Sauce, nacho cheese sauce, reduced-fat sour cream, and crispy potato bites, wrapped up in a flour tortilla and grilled. - New Chips & Nacho Supreme Dip (suggested price of $2.49) - Layers of seasoned beef, refried beans, nacho cheese sauce, reduced-fat sour cream, pico de gallo, and a three-cheese blend, served with tortilla chips. - New Avocado Ranch Chicken Stacker (suggested price of $2.99) - Grilled white-meat chicken, Avocado Ranch Sauce, three-cheese blend, lettuce, and tomatoes, folded in a flour tortilla and grilled. - New, limited-time Salted Caramel Churros (suggested price of $1.99) - Three churros with a creamy filling, dusted in salted caramel sugar. Returning on the value menu are: - Cheesy Roll Up for a suggested price of $1.19. - Spicy Potato Soft Taco for a suggested price of $1.29. - Cheesy Bean and Rice Burrito for a suggested price of $1.49. - 3 Cheese Chicken Flatbread Melt for a suggested price of $2.29. - Cheesy Double Beef Burrito for a suggested price of $2.79. As a promotion for the updated value menu, on January 27, 2026, at 2PM, Taco Bell will be holding a Tuesday Drop where the first 30,000 rewards members can claim a Luxe Value Menu item for $1. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Krispy Kreme Welcomes Winter Seasonal Collection Donuts
    Krispy Kreme introduces its new Winter Seasonal Collection of four donuts for the 2025-2026 winter. The winter lineup includes: - Caramel Dulce Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in caramel icing with a salted caramel dulce drizzle. - Chocolate Truffle Donut - A donut filled with chocolate truffle-flavored Kreme, dipped in chocolate icing, decorated with drizzles of chocolate icing, and finished with chocolate chips. - Raspberry Cheesecake Donut - A donut dipped in raspberry-flavored icing and graham crunch, topped with a rosette of cheesecake-flavored buttercreme. - Cinnamon Sugar Cake Donut - An Original Glazed, cinnamon old-fashioned cake donut. Krispy Kreme also offers two seasonal beverages this winter: the Caramel Vanilla Latte and the Caramel Brown Sugar Latte. Both are available hot, iced, or frozen. Krispy Kreme's Winter Seasonal Collection is available now for a limited time at participating locations across the US. Additionally, from January 16 through January 19, 2026, Kripsy Kreme is offering a deal where you can get a dozen Original Glazed donuts for $1 with the price of any regular-priced dozen. When ordering online, you'll need to use the code "BOGO1". Photo via Krispy Kreme. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Review: KFC - Spicy Zinger Sandwich
    KFC's Spicy Zinger Sandwich features a new Extra Crispy Spicy Zinger marinated chicken breast filet served with shredded Romaine lettuce and the Colonel's Mayo on a toasted brioche bun. It's a test item here in California. I bought one for $6.49. I think my local KFC was still working out the kinks on making this (or they just didn't have lettuce on hand), as mine came with pickles instead of lettuce. I didn't mind it as lettuce on a hot chicken sandwich tends to wilt quickly, while pickles add a nice crunch and acidity. In terms of texture, the new chicken filet felt the same as KFC's regular Extra Crispy chicken breast filet. Mine felt like it had been sitting in the warmer for a while, but it was thick, relatively tender, and moist inside and flaky and crispy outside. Flavor-wise, there was a light seasoned flavor and a respectable amount of medium heat. It was spicy enough to get my nose running, but not so much that I went reaching for milk. It's probably as spicy as you're going to get at a major fast-food chain that isn't dishing Buffalo wings. The bun was a little stale. It was still moist inside, but the crust was somewhat hardened and dry. Overall, KFC's Spicy Zinger Sandwich was pretty good and even better if you can get a fresh one. I enjoyed the heat, and I prefer a spicy chicken filet over a spicy sauce. Note - Sorry, there's no cross-section shot for this review. I cut the sandwich and set up the shot, but then had a brain fart and didn't take a picture. Nutritional Info - KFC Spicy Zinger Sandwich Calories - 480 Fat - 23g (Saturated Fat - 3g) Sodium - 1360mg Carbs - 37g (Sugar - 5g) Protein - 33g Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Little Caesars Launches New Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix
    Little Caesars introduces its new Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix as a limited-time offer that's a twist on the chain's regular-menu Slices-N-Stix. Positioned as a single-serve meal, the Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix features two slices of Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza, four pieces of Italian Cheese Bread, and Crazy Sauce for dipping. You can consider it as either half a Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza that's cut and topped like Italian Cheese Bread on one half or as an order of Italian Cheese Bread that's cut and topped like a Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza on one half. The Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix is available now for $4.99 (it's $5.99 here in California). Photo via Little Caesars. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Bojangles Debuts New Breakfast Bowl and Brings Back Chicken Rice Bowl
    Bojangles rings in the new year with the introduction of its new Breakfast Bowl and the return of the Chicken Rice Bowl. The new Breakfast Bowl is available all day and features country-style sausage, Bo-Rounds (fried potato coins similar to tater tots), eggs, Southern sausage gravy, and Monterey Jack cheese. The returning Chicken Rice Bowl features a bed of Bojangles Dirty Rice slow-cooked Cajun Pintos topped with a shredded Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheese blend and a choice of chicken (grilled chicken, two Chicken Supremes, or five BoBites), served with Texas Pete hot sauce on the side. Both the Breakfast Bowl and Chicken Rice Bowl are available now for a limited time at participating Bojangles locations. Prices vary, but a location in Atlanta, GA sells the Breakfast Bowl for $5.99 and the Chicken Rice Bowl for $7.49. Other limited-time specials at Bojangles right now include two-for-$4 Sausage Biscuits and the Salted Caramel Apple Crisp introduced last fall. Photo via Bojangles. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Pizza Hut Offers $10 Big New Yorker Deal
    Pizza Hut is running a limited-time deal where you can get its Big New Yorker pizza for $10 at participating locations nationwide. The Big New Yorker is an extra-large (16-inch) New York-style pizza dusted with parmesan-oregano seasoning and cut into six foldable slices. For $10 ($10.99 here in Los Angeles), you can get it with one topping of your choice or as a Pepperoni Duo (topped with crispy cupped pepperoni and regular pepperoni). Additional toppings and extra cheese are available for an upcharge. Photo via Pizza Hut. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    One country offers the world's cheapest eggs, with cartons selling for under $1 -- here's why prices are so low compared to costs in the United States.  ( 4 min )
    Review: Starbucks Mr Beast Cannon Ball Drink Makes A Colorful Splash
    Starbucks has released the Cannon Ball Drink to tie in with season 2 of "Beast Games." Here's everything to know about MrBeast's new lemonade refresher.  ( 6 min )
    Here Are The Best Jewish Delis You Can Find In The US

    How Waffle House's Hashbrown Code Became A Fixture At The Breakfast Chain

    Save Your Bread Crusts For Easy Cinnamon Rolls In No Time

    How Long Should You Steam Couscous? Here's The Ideal Time
    Couscous isn't as popular as it could be because most people who are unfamiliar with the dish don't feel confident about cooking it. It's really quite simple.  ( 4 min )

    Why Some Vegetables Are Healthier Cooked Than Raw
    The raw diet craze has died done a little bit, but the science behind cooking food is robust. Here's why some vegetables are healthier when cooked.  ( 4 min )
    For The Easiest Loaded Baked Potatoes Grab This Type Of Baking Pan

  • Open

    $6, $8, and $10 Gotta Habit Meal Deals are Back at Habit Burger
    Gotta Habit Meal Deals are back for a limited time at Habit Burger & Grill with prices ranging from $6 to $10. The options include: - $10 Gotta Habit Meal Deal (you can find my previous review of it here)- Half a Santa Barbara Char sandwich, five Crispy Chicken Bites, a side of your choice (choose from Tempura Green Beans, French Fries, or a Side Garden Salad with Ranch), and a large drink. - $8 Gotta Habit Meal Deal - Half a Chicken Club sandwich, a side of your choice (choose from Tempura Green Beans, French Fries, or a Side Garden Salad with Ranch), and a large drink. - $6 Gotta Habit Meal Deal - Half a BBQ Chicken Sandwich, a side of your choice (choose from Tempura Green Beans, French Fries, or a Side Garden Salad with Ranch), and a regular drink. The discounted combo meals are available now at participating locations. Photo via Habit Burger & Grill. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    New Little Debbie Ice Cream Sandwiches Land in Stores
    Snack cake brand Little Debbie partners with Hudsonville Ice Cream to launch new Little Debbie Ice Cream Sandwiches. The new dessert comes in two flavors:  - Oatmeal Creme Pies Ice Cream Sandwiches - Vanilla crème flavored ice cream sandwiched between two oatmeal cookies - Cosmic Brownies Ice Cream Sandwiches - Brownie-flavored ice cream sandwiched between two chewy fudge brownies with rainbow chips Little Debbie Ice Cream Sandwiches are rolling out to Kroger, Meijer, Hy-Vee, Giant Eagle, Mariano's, Roundy's, Family Fare, Tony's Fresh Market, Strack & Van Til Food Market, Berkot's Super Foods, and Hannaford, with the potential for more retailers to be added later. The new ice cream sandwiches join a Little Debbie frozen treat line that also includes pints and ice cream bars. Photo via Hudsonville Ice Cream. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Burger King Offers $4.99 Double Cheeseburger Meal
    Burger King offers the $4.99 Double Cheeseburger Meal as a limited-time value meal deal at participating locations nationwide. The discounted meal features a Double Cheeseburger, value-size fries, and a value-size fountain drink. The price may vary. It costs $5.99 here in Los Angeles. The Double Cheeseburger features two flame-grilled beef patties (over a quarter-pound pre-cooked), American cheese, pickles, yellow mustard, and ketchup, on a toasted sesame seed bun. Photo via Burger King. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Fish Sandwiches are Back at Arby's
    Fish sandwiches make their annual return to Arby's for the 2026 Lenten season. This year's lineup includes the Crispy Fish Sandwich and King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe. The Crispy Fish Sandwich is a classic fish sandwich consisting of a crispy-fried fish filet served with shredded lettuce and tartar sauce on a toasted sesame bun. A slice of cheddar and/or bacon can be added for an additional charge. The King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe features A crispy-fried fish filet with cheddar cheese, shredded lettuce, tomato, and tartar sauce on a toasted King's Hawaiian roll. Prices vary, but the Crispy Fish Sandwich costs $5.69 at a location here in Los Angeles, while the King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe goes for $6.69. Other limited-time items at Arby's right now include the Italian Beef Dip, Apple Pie Shake, and Peach Cobbler Roll. The fast-food chain also has a limited-time, discounted combo meal called the "Meat & 3 Box." Photo via Arby's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Volcano Quesarito is Back at Taco Bell
    The Volcano Quesarito is back for a limited time at Taco Bell with a slight tweak: new Volcano Sauce. Additionally, the chain introduces the Rewards Member-exclusive Volcano Style Menu featuring various regular menu items with new Volcano Sauce added. Taco Bell describes its new Volcano Sauce as a "creamy, craveable sauce made with a blend of cheese and flavorful spices, delivering the heat legacy of Lava Sauce that fans know and love." It's positioned as a "bold evolution of fan-favorite Lava Sauce." You can find it in the Volcano Quesarito and featured in the online-only Volcano Style Menu. Volcano Sauce can also be added to any menu item or ordered on the side. The Volcano Quesarito (here's my previous review from when it cost $2.69 and contained Lava Sauce instead of Volcano Sauce) features a grilled quesadilla with melted cheese, nacho cheese sauce, and new Volcano Sauce wrapped around a filling of seasoned ground beef, seasoned rice, and reduced-fat sour cream. It carries a suggested price of $4.99 (it's $5.49 at my local Taco Bell here in Los Angeles). The Volcano Style Menu can be found under the "Fan Style" tab on the Taco Bell website and mobile app when signed into your Taco Bell Rewards account. It includes: - Volcano Crunchy Taco - A Crunchy Taco with Volcano Sauce added. - Volcano Crunchwrap - A Crunchwrap Supreme with Volcano Sauce added. - Volcano Cheesy G - A Cheesy Gordita Crunch with Volcano Sauce instead of Spicy Ranch. - Volcano Steak Fries - Steak Garlic Nacho Fries with Volcano Sauce instead of Creamy Garlic Sauce. - Volcano BellGrande - Nachos Bell Grande with Volcano Sauce added. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Burger King Debuts New Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper and Fried Apple Pie
    Burger King introduces the new Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper, the fast-food chain's latest fan-inspired creation submitted through the "Whopper by You" platform, which encourages fans to submit their own Whopper ideas. Burger King also welcomes a new fried Cinnamon Apple Pie. The Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper features a 4.4-oz flame-grilled beef patty served with crispy onion rings, bacon, sautéed mushrooms, Swiss cheese, and creamy peppercorn aioli on a toasted sesame seed bun. A Jr. version is also available. Prices for the new Whopper vary, but it goes for $10.89 at my local BK here in Los Angeles (the Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper Jr. costs $6.29). Burger King has used the "steakhouse" moniker for quite a few burgers in the past. Some examples include the Steakhouse Whopper, the Steakhouse King, and the BK Stuffed Steakhouse Burger. The new, limited-time Cinnamon Apple Pie is a deep-fried turnover-style pie with a crispy crust and warm, cinnamon apple filling. It costs $1.99 near me. The chain previously offered the Dutch Apple Pie, which was sold by the slice and discontinued in 2020. The Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper follows last year's BBQ Brisket Whopper and Crispy Onion Whopper in the "Whopper by You" series. Burger King plans to release additional guest-inspired Whopper burgers later this year. Photo via Burger King. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
